title: Using VSET
description:
Using VSET

Using VSet

VSet sets a property value at runtime. This allows developers to dynamically adjust property settings from a SPEL+ program.  In most cases you can set property values from the Vision Guide window and then run vision sequences from SPEL+ programs with no modifications of the properties.  However, for those cases which require dynamic adjustments, the VSET SPEL+ command can be used.

Here is an example of using VSet in a SPEL+ program.  Notice that the first call to VSet sets a sequence property.  The second call to VSet sets an object property called SearchWin which is used to redefine the position and size of a search window before running the sequence.

Function findPart

  ' Set camera brightness for sequence "findPart"

  VSet findPart.camerabrightness, 100

  ' Set search window for object "part"

  VSet findPart.part.searchWin, 100, 100, 50, 50

  ' Run the sequenced

  VRun findPart

Fend
examples:
Function findPart ' Set camera brightness for sequence "findPart" VSet findPart.camerabrightness, 100 ' Set search window for object "part" VSet findPart.part.searchWin, 100, 100, 50, 50 ' Run the sequenced VRun findPart
Fend
